depression.description: I often feel sad thinking about how my family reacted when I came out. It makes me feel unlovable and like I'll never receive the support I need from them.
Even though I moved to New York to find myself, I often feel incredibly lonely. I miss the idea of family support, but I know I can't go back to how things were.
Whenever I think about telling my parents about my breakup, I get overwhelmed. I can't shake the feeling that they won't care about my feelings and will only focus on how it reflects on them.
I find myself emotionally distancing from my family to protect myself, but it leaves me feeling sad and disconnected. I wish I could feel closer to them without the fear of rejection.

Delusion.description: I feel like I need my family's approval to feel good about myself. It's exhausting to chase after their acceptance, especially when I know they might never give it.
I'm terrified of going through another experience like coming out. The thought of facing their rejection again makes me want to avoid sharing anything personal with them.
